what: taxify: Offline Taxonomic Name Matching Against Local Darwin Core Snapshots — taxify-package
title: taxify: Offline Taxonomic Name Matching Against Local Darwin Core Snapshots — taxify-package
text: Match taxonomic names locally stored Darwin Core backbone databases ('WFO', 'COL', 'GBIF', 'ITIS', 'NCBI Taxonomy', 'Open Tree Life', 'WoRMS', 'Species Fungorum', 'AlgaeBase'). Provides offline fuzzy exact matching synonym resolution, hybrid name detection, unified output schema across sources. heavy computation runs 'vectra' C11 columnar engine.

title: taxify: Offline Taxonomic Name Matching Against Local Darwin Core Snapshots — taxify-package
text: Maintainer: Gilles Colling gilles.colling051@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder] Authors: Gilles Colling gilles.colling051@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder]

what: Match taxonomic names against local backbone databases — taxify
title: Match taxonomic names against local backbone databases — taxify
text: Matches vector taxonomic names locally stored Darwin Core backbone databases. Returns data.frame one row per input name containing matched name, accepted name, taxonomic hierarchy, match quality information.

title: Match taxonomic names against local backbone databases — taxify
code: taxify(   x,   backend = "wfo",   fuzzy = TRUE,   fuzzy_threshold = 0.2,   fuzzy_method = c("dl", "levenshtein", "jw"),   aggregates = c("preserve", "collapse"),   region = NULL,   coords = NULL,   range = c("present", "native", "introduced"),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Match taxonomic names against local backbone databases — taxify
text: x Character vector taxonomic names. backend Character vector backend names (e.g., "wfo", "col", "gbif") single taxify_backend object. multiple backends given, tried order fallback chain. Default "wfo". fuzzy Logical. Enable fuzzy matching names fail exact match. Default TRUE. fuzzy_threshold Numeric. Maximum allowed distance fuzzy matches. Two modes depending value: Fractional (0 < fuzzy_threshold < 1): normalized distance (edits / max name length). Default 0.2 1 edit per 5 characters. Integer (fuzzy_threshold >= 1): maximum raw edit count, e.g. fuzzy_threshold = 2L allows 2 insertions/deletions/substitutions regardless name length. supported fuzzy_method = "jw". fuzzy_method Character. One "dl" (Damerau-Levenshtein, default), "levenshtein", "jw" (Jaro-Winkler). aggregates Character. treat species aggregates (names agg. / s.l. qualifier). "preserve" (default) keeps aggregate concept: matches backbone's aggregate taxon ("<binomial> aggr.") one exists, otherwise falls back binomial. "collapse" strips marker matches binomial species, way non-aggregate name matched. Either way qualifier recorded qualifier column. region TDWG botanical region(s) constrain fuzzy matching , NULL (default) geographic constraint. Accepts Level 3 codes ("BGM", c("BGM", "GER")) region names level, matched case- accent-insensitively bundled WGSRPD crosswalk: Level 3 name ("Belgium"), Level 2 region ("Middle Europe"), Level 1 continent ("Europe", expands codes). See taxify_regions() full list. set, fuzzy candidates restricted species WCVP records region(s); exact matches always kept. filter narrows genuinely ambiguous fuzzy candidates: candidate dropped input name another candidate -region WCVP range data, non-plant matches (WCVP coverage) never affected name whose candidate --region still returned. WCVP vascular plants , disambiguates plant names. coords Coordinates constrain fuzzy matching , mapped TDWG regions point--polygon unioned region. single c(lon, lat) pair, matrix/data.frame longitude/latitude columns (named lon/lat x/y, else first two columns lon, lat), point-geometry spatial object (sf/sfc object terra SpatVector, reprojected longitude/latitude automatically). NULL (default) none. WGSRPD boundary file downloaded cached; coordinate lookup needs download (prior cache). point-- polygon test uses terra sf installed, otherwise native fallback; force engine options(taxify.pip_engine = "terra" | "sf" | "native"). range Character. WCVP statuses count -region region coords set. "present" (default) accepts record (native, introduced, extinct) – right choice name disambiguation. "native" accepts native records, "introduced" introduced (alien) records; fold ecological filter matching callers want . Ignored region set. verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default TRUE.

title: Match taxonomic names against local backbone databases — taxify
text: data.frame one row per input name following columns: input_name original name provided. matched_name Full name backbone matched. accepted_name Resolved accepted name (equals matched_name synonym). taxon_id Backend-specific ID matched name. accepted_id ID accepted name. rank Taxonomic rank (species, subspecies, genus, etc.). family Family name. genus Genus name. epithet Specific epithet. authorship Authorship matched name. accepted_authorship Authorship accepted name. synonym author resolved accepted name, synonym's author, accepted_name accepted_authorship together form accepted name's full citation. is_synonym Logical. match synonym? is_hybrid Logical. hybrid marker detected input? qualifier Canonical taxonomic qualifier found input name ("cf.", "aff.", "agg.", "s.l.", "s.str.", "sp.", ...), NA. Spelling variants folded one token ("aggr.", "agg" "sensu lato" map "agg."/"s.l."). qualifier_position "genus" qualifier leads name qualifies whole name (e.g. "Cf. Pinus sylvestris"), "species" qualifies species (inline cf. trailing agg.), NA qualifier. match_type One "exact", "exact_ci", "fuzzy", "abbrev" (abbreviated genus "Q. robur" resolved via genus initial plus epithet), "none". fuzzy_dist Normalized string distance (0–1), NA exact. is_ambiguous Logical. TRUE matched scientificName multiple synonym rows pointing different accepted taxa priority tier (homonym ambiguity). Disambiguated via nomenclaturalStatus = "Valid" column backbone; irreducible ambiguity, scalar columns hold one candidate. ambiguous_targets Character. |-joined list conflicting accepted taxon IDs is_ambiguous = TRUE; NA otherwise. backend backend used (e.g., "wfo", "col", "gbif"). backbone_version Backend name, version, download date (e.g., "wfo:2024-12 (2026-04-01)"). Useful reproducibility.

title: Match taxonomic names against local backbone databases — taxify
text: multiple backends specified, names matched backend order. Names matched earlier backend re-matched later ones (fallback chain).

title: Match taxonomic names against local backbone databases — taxify
code: # Runs offline against the bundled example database. old <- options(taxify.data_dir = taxify_example_data())  # Match a few names taxify(c("Quercus robur", "Pinus sylvestris"))  # Disable fuzzy matching taxify("Quercus robus", fuzzy = FALSE)  # Constrain fuzzy candidates to a geographic region: a TDWG Level 3 code, # or a region name resolved via the bundled WGSRPD crosswalk taxify("Quercus robus", region = "EUR") taxify("Quercus robus", region = "Belgium")  # Constrain by coordinates (downloads WGSRPD boundaries on first use) if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ taxify("Quercus robus", coords = c(4.35, 50.85)) } # }  # Fallback chain: try WFO first, then COL for unmatched taxify(c("Quercus robur", "Panthera leo"),        backend = c("wfo", "col"))  options(old)

title: Reshape grouped enrichment columns to long format — taxify_long
text: x data.frame, typically taxify() result applying grouped enrichment like add_invasive_status(), add_alien_first_records(), add_wcvp(). cols Character vector base column names reshape. column names without group suffix (e.g., "invasive_status", "invasive_status_AT"). omitted, auto-detected enrichment metadata stamped add_*() functions. group_col Character. Name output group column. omitted, auto-detected enrichment metadata (e.g., "country_code" invasive status alien first records). drop_na Logical. TRUE, drop rows value columns NA. Default FALSE.

title: Reshape grouped enrichment columns to long format — taxify_long
text: data.frame long format. columns x part reshape preserved. reshaped columns use base names (without suffix), new group_col column contains group code extracted suffix.

title: Reshape grouped enrichment columns to long format — taxify_long
text: cols group_col omitted, taxify_long() reads reshape metadata attached grouped enrichment functions (add_invasive_status(), add_alien_first_records(), add_wcvp(), add_common_names()). multiple grouped enrichments applied, reshaped together (must share group column). Column matching uses explicit base names cols avoid ambiguity. example, given cols = c("alien_first_record", "alien_first_record_source"), column alien_first_record_source_AT correctly matched base alien_first_record_source (alien_first_record suffix source_AT), longer base names matched first. columns x exactly match cols (suffixed variants), data already single-group format. case, data.frame returned unchanged group_col set NA.

title: Reshape grouped enrichment columns to long format — taxify_long
code: # Runs offline against the bundled example database. old <- options(taxify.data_dir = taxify_example_data())  # Auto-detected: no cols or group_col needed taxify("Robinia pseudoacacia") |>   add_alien_first_records(country = c("AT", "DE")) |>   taxify_long()  # Explicit: override auto-detection taxify("Robinia pseudoacacia") |>   add_invasive_status(country = c("AT", "DE")) |>   taxify_long(cols = "invasive_status", group_col = "country")  options(old)

title: taxify 0.3.1
text: New reptiledb backend: Reptile Database (Uetz et al.), global taxonomic reference reptiles (snakes, lizards, amphisbaenians, turtles, crocodiles tuatara). carries ~12.6k accepted species plus ~34k synonyms full genus/family classification, filling one vertebrate class backbones cover partially. Use like backbone: taxify(x, backend = "reptiledb"). License: CC-4.0. New add_repttraits() joins species-level reptile traits ReptTraits (Oskyrko et al. 2024) accepted name. Beyond body-size life-history traits, carries per-species distribution signal – biogeographic realm, elevation range mean climate – across reptiles. replaces earlier add_lizard_traits(), drew ReptTraits source mislabelled (covered reptiles, lizards) exposed morphology columns. License: CC-4.0. New inspect() flags probable typos anomalies name list returns anomalous rows, labelled stands , known, name use instead. inspect() match names backbones – taxify()‘s job. character vector runs checks need matching: unknown (genus genus register, union 13 backbones’ genera, backbone recognises – real “probably name”), near_duplicate (near-twin frequent name list, computed list alone, catches typos names backbone contains), outlier_group (name whose kingdom group tiny minority otherwise coherent list – lone animal fungus among plants). also surface match-based anomalies, opt backbones = TRUE (matches every installed backbone, listed report header) match first inspect result – taxify(x) |> inspect() – adds typo (fuzzy-corrected spelling), synonym (outdated name), ambiguous (homonym), case, geographic checks geographic (species WCVP record declared region/coords) out_of_range (region declared, yet species’ range falls outside list’s main TDWG continents; skipped globally spread lists). Rows ordered -notable first carry suggestion; gets tier describing needs, bad : unresolved (usable name), review (name identity uncertain), note (correct, optional cleanup) – anomaly may intended. inspect() read-: never alters input applies correction. Narrow min_tier. list-context labels apply single name, inspect() one name warns. taxify() gains region argument geographically constrained fuzzy matching. Pass TDWG botanical regions restrict fuzzy candidates species WCVP records regions; exact matches always kept. region accepts Level 3 codes (region = "BGM") region names level, matched case- accent-insensitively bundled WGSRPD crosswalk: botanical country ("Belgium"), Level 2 region ("Middle Europe"), Level 1 continent ("Europe", expanded codes). new coords argument takes c(lon, lat) pair matrix/data.frame coordinates maps regions point--polygon WGSRPD Level 3 boundaries (downloaded cached first use); region coords unioned. coords also accepts sf/sfc object terra SpatVector points (reprojected automatically). point-- polygon test uses terra sf installed falls back native implementation otherwise; engine can forced options(taxify.pip_engine = "terra" | "sf" | "native"). filter narrows genuinely ambiguous fuzzy candidates – candidate dropped input name another candidate -region WCVP range data – non-plant matches never affected name whose candidate --region still returned. companion range argument selects WCVP statuses count -region: "present" (default, record), "native", "introduced". New taxify_regions() lists WGSRPD Level 3 botanical regions (codes names) used region add_wcvp(), optional search term. Two new backbone backends: "fishbase" (FishBase, ~36k accepted fish species) "sealifebase" (SeaLifeBase, ~100k accepted non-fish aquatic species). resolve synonyms accepted names carry kingdom / phylum / class / order classification. Use like backbone, e.g. taxify("Gadus morhua", backend = "fishbase"). add_sealifebase() joins SeaLifeBase morphological ecological traits (body length, mass, trophic level, depth range, vulnerability, habitat, commercial importance) taxify() result. non-fish companion add_fishbase(). add_groot() joins species-level root traits Global Root Traits (GRooT) database: root diameter, specific root length, tissue density, N C concentration, root mass fraction, lateral spread, mycorrhizal colonization rooting depth (per-species means 6,154 vascular plant species).

title: taxify 0.3.0
text: add_qualifier_info() removed. taxify() now reports qualifier natively (see New features), separate parsing pass longer needed. Replace taxify(x) |> add_qualifier_info() taxify(x). Note integer qualifier_position (character index) replaced two-value "genus" / "species" placement.

title: taxify 0.3.0
text: Species aggregates now handled distinct concept. taxify() gains aggregates argument, default "preserve": aggregate name ("... agg.", "... s.l.") matches backbone’s aggregate taxon one exists (example Euro+Med WoRMS), otherwise falls back binomial species. aggregates = "collapse" keeps previous behaviour stripping marker matching binomial. taxify() output carries two new columns. qualifier canonical taxonomic qualifier spelling variants folded one token ("aggr.", "agg" "sensu lato" map "agg." / "s.l."). qualifier_position "genus" leading prefix ("Cf. Pinus sylvestris") "species" inline trailing qualifier ("Pinus cf. sylvestris", "Rubus fruticosus agg."). Trait enrichment aggregate-aware. Traits inherit taxonomic hierarchy: species query receives aggregate-level trait species-level value exists (example EIVE indicator values keyed "... aggr."), aggregate query reaches aggregate-level trait. single species’ trait never propagated aggregate. Set options(taxify.trait_provenance = TRUE) add <enrichment>_inherited flag marking inherited values. Exported normalize_aggregate_name() is_aggregate_name() (build-time helpers) taxifydb build pipeline folds every backbone enrichment aggregate marker (agg, aggr., -agg, s.l., sensu lato, coll., aggregate ranks SPECIES AGGREGATE) one canonical aggr. form. runtime build sides therefore recognize aggregates uniformly across backbones.

title: taxify 0.2.15
text: summary() now counts abbreviated-genus matches (match_type == "abbrev", e.g. "Q. robur"). Previously resolved correctly result omitted matched total breakdown, digest -reported number matched names. matched line now reads (exact, case-insensitive, fuzzy, abbrev), match_tally carries abbrev count.

title: taxify 0.2.15
text: Rewrote “Getting started” vignette demo-first walkthrough: one-call example, four canvas animations (matching pipeline, genus blocking, WorldFlora speed benchmark, enrichment hub), worked trait-stacking analysis. section links dedicated vignette depth. enrichments vignette now documents add_fungalroot() (genus-level mycorrhizal type), including genus-keyed join type vocabulary, stacks European global vascular-plant guidance.

title: taxify 0.2.14
text: add_fungalroot() joins genus-level mycorrhizal type FungalRoot database (Soudzilovskaia et al. 2020, GBIF doi:10.15468/a7ujmj, CC -NC 4.0) taxify() result. mycorrhizal type conserved genus level, join genus, species covered genus annotated mycorrhizal_type (, EcM, ErM, OM, NM, dual types, , uncertain), mycorrhizal_status, supporting record count. Plant genera .

title: taxify 0.2.13
text: leading genus-level Cf. prefix (e.g. "Cf. Pinus sylvestris") now recorded cf. qualifier clean_names(), clean_one(), add_qualifier_info(). Previously prefix stripped matching qualifier lost, inline cf. (e.g. "Pinus cf. sylvestris") reported. add_qualifier_info() now also matches prefix case-insensitively reports qualifier_position = 1.

title: taxify 0.2.12
text: taxify_data_dir() can now redirected taxify.data_dir option TAXIFY_DATA_DIR environment variable, cache location configurable (shared caches, scratch directories, bundled example data). taxify_example_data() returns path small bundled example database (handful species per backbone plus matching enrichment tables). Setting options(taxify.data_dir = taxify_example_data()) lets matching enrichment run fully offline.

title: taxify 0.2.12
text: Examples now run bundled example database instead wrapped \dontrun{}. add_pignatti() (fetched live via TR8) list_enrichments() (reads online manifest) remain \donttest{}.

title: taxify 0.2.11
text: add_floraweb() joins German-flora plant traits FloraWeb (live BfN portal carrying BiolFlor data Klotz, Kuehn & Durka 2002, plus Rothmaler morphology Ellenberg indicator values). bundles full per-species trait profile – morphology, reproductive biology, nine Ellenberg indicator values, ploidy chromosome number, chorological distribution (59 _de columns) – pre-built dataset, works offline.

title: taxify 0.2.11
text: add_ecoflora() now joins bundled, pre-built Ecoflora dataset (18 _uk columns: canopy height, leaf traits, life form, flowering phenology, pollination, seed weight, British-calibrated Ellenberg values) instead fetching live TR8. works offline returns full trait set rather previous five columns. add_pignatti() remains -demand TR8 source: values copyrighted publication redistributed.

title: taxify 0.2.10
text: add_ecoflora(), add_biolflor(), add_pignatti() join plant traits taxify ship pre-built dataset, accessing demand suggested TR8 package machine; taxify redistributes nothing. reasons differ source: add_ecoflora() adds British flowering months, pollen vector, life form, leaf longevity (CC -NC-SA, allow redistribution, ecoflora.org.uk bulk download, fetched live per species); add_biolflor() adds Grime CSR strategy type, breeding system, pollen vector, life form, life span, apomixis (usable acknowledgement + citation per BioFresh metadata statement, bulk copy obtainable UFZ site offline, fetched live); add_pignatti() adds Italian Ellenberg-type indicator values, life form, chorotype (copyrighted; read copy bundled TR8, TR8 redistributes, taxify; works offline). Columns region-suffixed (_uk/_de/_it) never collide add_baseflor(). TR8 Suggests dependency. live source (Ecoflora, BiolFlor) unreachable call errors rather attaching silent NA.

title: taxify 0.2.9
text: add_baseflor() joins plant traits Baseflor (Programme Catminat, Julve 1998 ff.; ODbL 1.0 / CC -SA 2.0) taxify() result. covers ~7,000 vascular plant taxa France neighbouring regions adds flowering phenology (flower_begin_month, flower_end_month), pollination vector, dispersal mode, breeding system, flower colour, fruit type, woody growth form, continentality salinity indicator-value axes absent EIVE. enrichment registered manifest (list_enrichments()) pre-built .vtr; light/temperature/moisture/reaction/nutrient axes left add_eive() Raunkiaer life form add_leda().

title: taxify 0.2.8
text: Added end--end regression test (tests/e2e/test-e2e-enrichment.R) enrichment join fixed 0.2.5 (#1). checks add_conservation_status(), add_common_names(), add_woodiness() attach value row’s accepted taxon, stay invariant batch composition order, land documented values correct species.

title: taxify 0.2.7
text: Abbreviated-genus names "Q. robur" now resolve. matching pass restricts backbone rows whose genus starts given initial whose specific epithet matches, resolving unique. two genera sharing initial also share epithet abbreviation ambiguous: row left unmatched is_ambiguous = TRUE conflicting accepted IDs ambiguous_targets, rather guessing genus. genus spelled full elsewhere input takes precedence (convention abbreviating first mention). Resolved rows carry match_type = "abbrev".

title: taxify 0.2.6
text: New accepted_authorship output column: authorship resolved accepted name. synonym match, authorship holds synonym’s author accepted_authorship holds accepted name’s author, accepted_name accepted_authorship together form accepted taxon’s full citation. Backbones carry authorship populate ; sources without authorship (NCBI, OTT) return NA.

title: taxify 0.2.6
text: taxify() longer errors “replacement length zero” backbones whose .meta sidecar records build date build_date (current taxifydb build format) rather download_date. Backbone metadata now reads layouts version formatting tolerates missing date. previously broke matching WoRMS Open Tree Life backbones.

title: taxify 0.2.6
text: Declared companion build package taxifydb Additional_repositories (https://gcol33.r-universe.dev), location discoverable required Suggests dependency outside mainstream repositories.

title: taxify 0.2.5
text: taxify() longer errors “incorrect number dimensions” genus register present backend-coverage file (state clean install coverage download, package checks). early return() evaluated inside tryCatch() expression returned NULL pre-filter, $<- turned list; --scope pre-filter now resolves missing coverage -op preserves result data frame. Replaced non-ASCII characters roxygen documentation ASCII equivalents PDF reference manual builds LaTeX.

title: taxify 0.2.4
text: Ambiguous homonym synonyms now resolve epithet-preserving accepted name (homotypic basionym) instead arbitrary lowest-id candidate. taxify("Pinus abies") resolves Picea abies (Picea polita), spurious is_ambiguous flag cleared one candidate keeps specific epithet (#2). Genuinely ambiguous names (candidate, several, preserving epithet) still flagged. Silenced tidyselect deprecation warnings emitted fuzzy matching.

title: taxify 0.2.4
text: score_candidates() exported (kept internal reference index) companion taxifydb build pipeline can collapse backbone key single accepted name taxify() resolves . corrects enrichment joins previously landed trait/status values within-genus neighbours (#1); fix reaches users rebuilt enrichment data.
